messages.content: Send a follow up email regarding a job position

Subject: Reiterating Interest and Appreciation for Job Position Opportunity

Dear [Hiring Manager's Name],

I hope this email finds you in good health and high spirits. I am writing to follow up on my recent application for the [Job Position] at [Company Name]. I wanted to reiterate my strong interest in the role and express my gratitude for the opportunity to be considered.

Since submitting my application, I have continued to research and learn more about [Company Name]. I am genuinely captivated by the company's impressive achievements, innovative approach, and commitment to [specific industry/mission]. Aligning my skills and career aspirations with [Company Name] is both exciting and motivating for me.

My extensive experience in [relevant industry/field], as outlined in my resume, has provided me with a solid foundation and an ability to tackle the challenges associated with the [Job Position]. With proven success in [mention specific relevant achievements], I am confident in my ability to contribute towards [Company Name]'s ongoing growth and success.

I would be thrilled to discuss how my skills and experience can be an asset to your team. Please let me know if there is any additional information or documentation I can provide to support my application. I am readily available for a conversation or an interview at your convenience.

Once again, I deeply appreciate the opportunity to be considered for the [Job Position]. I firmly believe that my passion, qualifications, and dedication make me a strong contender. I eagerly await your response and the chance to further discuss how I can contribute to [Company Name].

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
[Your Contact Information]
Send a 3 follow up email regarding a job position

Follow-Up Email 1:

Subject: Checking in on the Status of my Job Application

Dear [Hiring Manager’s Name],

I hope this email finds you well. I wanted to follow up on my application for the [Job Position] at [Company Name], which I submitted on [Date]. I am very interested in the opportunity to join your esteemed organization and contribute my skills and experience.

I understand that you may still be in the process of reviewing applications. However, I wanted to assure you of my continued enthusiasm for the position. I have thoroughly researched [Company Name] and remain impressed by its accomplishments, values, and culture. The prospect of joining such a dynamic team is truly exciting for me.

I would appreciate it if you could provide me with an update on the status of my application. If there are any additional documents or information you require, please let me know, and I will be happy to provide them promptly.

Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to the possibility of discussing my qualifications further and hopefully, the opportunity to contribute to [Company Name]'s success.

Kind regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Contact Information]

Follow-Up Email 2:

Subject: Reiterating My Keen Interest in the [Job Position]

Dear [Hiring Manager’s Name],

I hope all is well. It has been a week since I submitted my application for the [Job Position] at [Company Name], and I wanted to check in and reiterate my strong interest in the role.

Considering the alignment between my experience, skills, and the requirements outlined in the job description, I firmly believe that I can make a meaningful contribution to [Company Name]. Moreover, after delving deeper into the latest developments in the [specific industry/field], my enthusiasm for joining your team has only grown stronger.

I truly appreciate the time and effort you dedicate to reviewing applications, and I understand that the selection process can be demanding. I want to assure you that, if invited for an interview, I would be fully prepared to highlight how my insights, dedication, and achievements can benefit [Company Name].

Thank you once again for your consideration. I look forward to the possibility of discussing my fit for the [Job Position] and contributing to [Company Name]'s growth and success.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Contact Information]

Follow-Up Email 3:

Subject: Finalizing my Application for the [Job Position]

Dear [Hiring Manager’s Name],

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to express my continued interest in the [Job Position] at [Company Name], as well as to inquire about any updates regarding my application.

Given my background in [relevant industry/field] and the passion I have for [specific industry/field], I am confident that my skills and expertise align well with the needs of [Company Name]. I am eager to contribute to a dynamic team focused on innovation and [specific industry/mission].

I understand that you may have numerous applications to review, but I would appreciate any information you can provide on the status of my application. Should there be any new or upcoming developments that affect the timeline for hiring decisions, I would also be grateful for an update.

Thank you for your time and consideration. I sincerely hope to have the opportunity to discuss my qualifications in person and demonstrate my commitment to making a positive impact at [Company Name].

Warmest regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Contact Information]

Enhance this sentence

I understand your point, and I originally thought about submitting this request asking if the 9/1 payment was sent since we were only 3 days into the month. But I decided to ask if the 10/1 payment was received to see if it had already been sent. 

Now with the understanding that the payment has not been received, and that we are now 4 days into the beginning of the month and the borrower has until the 15th to wire the payment is it okay if I back date the request asking if the 9/1 payment was received? So the borrower may receive the wire. 

I will also be relaying to the borrower to send the payment as soon as possible and to please confirm once payment has been sent. 

Please let me know if this works for you.

Thank you,

I understand your point, and originally I considered submitting a request to inquire if the 9/1 payment had been sent, given that we were only 3 days into the month. However, in order to check if the payment had already been sent, I decided to ask if the 10/1 payment was received.

Now, considering that the payment has not been received and we are already 4 days into the month, with the borrower having until the 15th to wire the payment, would it be acceptable if I back date the request to inquire if the 9/1 payment was received? This way, the borrower may receive the wire promptly.

Additionally, I will be advising the borrower to send the payment as soon as possible and kindly request confirmation once the payment has been sent.

Please let me know if this approach suits your requirements.

Thank you,
[Your Name]

Hi Steve,

I have received the final draw request from the borrower at 2581 Farley. The property is 100% completed and listed.

Would you consider subtracting both the 9/1 + late fee and the 10/1 payments from the request? 

Total breakdown for Monthly payments due for 9/1 and 10/1

9/1 Payment due:
Monthly Payment (1,200.00) * 5% Late fee =
•	(1,200) * (0.05) = 60.00
•	1,200.00) + 60.00 (late fee) = 1,260.00
•	Total due for 9/1 = 1,260.00

10/1 Payment due: 1,200.00

Total amount you would receive:

9/1 (1,260.00) + 10/1 (1,200.00)

1,260.00 + 1,200.00 = 2,460.00

Thank you,

Hi Steve,

I hope you're doing well. I wanted to inform you that I have received the final draw request from the borrower at 2581 Farley. The property is now 100% completed and listed.

In regards to the request, I propose subtracting both the 9/1 payment along with the corresponding late fee, as well as the 10/1 payment from the total requested amount. Here is the breakdown for the monthly payments due:

9/1 Payment due:
Monthly Payment ($1,200.00) * 5% Late fee = 
• ($1,200.00) * (0.05) = $60.00
• $1,200.00 + $60.00 (late fee) = $1,260.00
• Total due for 9/1 = $1,260.00

10/1 Payment due: $1,200.00

Therefore, the total amount you would receive is:
9/1 ($1,260.00) + 10/1 ($1,200.00) = $2,460.00

Please let me know if this proposal works for you. I appreciate your attention to this matter.

Thank you,
[Your Name]

Hi David,

Thanks for the update. I noticed two things:

First, there may have been a mistake on your end because the photos did not come through. 

Secondly, I noticed we don’t have a draw schedule on file. I have gone ahead created one for your review. Please rearrange items as needed so I can accurately tell what has been completed, and what order items will be completed in. 

Thank you,

Hi David,

Thank you for the update. I wanted to bring two things to your attention:

First, it seems there may have been an error on your end as the photos you mentioned did not come through. Could you please resend them so that I have a complete understanding of the situation?

Secondly, I noticed that we do not have a draw schedule on file. I have taken the initiative to create one for your review. Please take a look and feel free to rearrange the items as needed. This will allow us to accurately assess what has been completed and also determine the order in which the remaining items will be addressed.

Thank you for your attention to these matters. I look forward to receiving the photos and reviewing the updated draw schedule.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Hi David,

In the past you have provided us with a plentiful number of photos and if there weren’t 15 provided you would provide us with at minimum of 12. With that being said, company policy is a minimum of 15 but we have made exceptions, could you please provide us with at least 10 to 12 and for all future request 15minmimu.

Hi David,

I hope this email finds you well. I wanted to address a matter regarding the number of photos provided in the past. Typically, you have provided us with a generous amount, ensuring that we receive a minimum of 12 photos. However, our company policy stipulates a minimum requirement of 15 photos for each submission.

While we have made exceptions in the past, I kindly request that, for the current request and all future ones, you strictly adhere to the minimum requirement of 15 photos. This will ensure that we have a comprehensive and thorough understanding of the progress and any relevant details.

Thank you for your attention to this matter, and I appreciate your cooperation in meeting this guideline going forward.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Hi Sean,

I have spoken with management regarding your suggestion, and they are okay with making that change. 

Please understand this is a case by case instant, so doesn’t mean it will happen everytime. It was also relayed that you have been a great borrower and are confident in your success hence why they are okay with this change being made. 

I will circle back with you as soon as I receive an update on the wire being sent. 

Thank you

Hi Sean,

I hope this email finds you well. I wanted to update you regarding your suggestion. I have discussed it with the management team, and they have given their approval to make the change.

However, please note that this is a case-by-case decision and may not apply to every situation. It was also expressed that you have been a commendable borrower and that management has confidence in your success, which is why they agreed to this change.

I will keep you informed and get back to you as soon as I receive an update on the wire being sent.

Thank you for your understanding and cooperation.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Hi Kyle,

I have received confirmation that your request has been approved. 

You should see the wire hit you’re your account sometime tomorrow. 

I will circle back with you upon wires confirmation. 

Thank you,

Hi Kyle,

I hope this email finds you well. I wanted to inform you that I have received confirmation that your request has been approved.

You can expect the wire to hit your account tomorrow. Please keep an eye out for it.

Once I receive confirmation of the wire being processed, I will promptly follow up with you.

Thank you for your patience and cooperation throughout this process.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Hi Paul,

Thanks for reaching out. We have been in conversation with the lender on the release. He wants to ensure the insurance has been paid.

I have looped in April, please share with her if the payments have been made. 

@April Xu Please feel free to share any other information/or questions regarding this request. 

Thank you,

Hi Paul,

Thank you for reaching out. We have been in communication with the lender regarding the release, and they have expressed the need to ensure that the insurance has been paid.

To address this, I have looped in April. April, could you please confirm whether the payments for insurance have been made? Additionally, feel free to share any other relevant information or raise any questions regarding this request.

Thank you both for your prompt attention to this matter.

Kind regards,
[Your Name]

Hi Jason, 

Thanks for providing the video of the work completed. 

By any chance do you think the pool will be completed at the same time as you listing the property. 

Thank you,

Hi Jason,

Thank you for providing the video showcasing the work that has been completed. I appreciate the update.

I wanted to inquire if there is a possibility that the pool will be completed concurrently with the listing of the property. It would be ideal if potential buyers could see the completed pool when the property is listed.

Please let me know your thoughts on this matter.

Thank you,
[Your Name]

Hi Katrina,

Thanks for providing the photos and video for draw 3s items. 

I just had a few questions:
1.	Do you have a video or more photos displaying the drywall fully completed inside the property
2.	With the roofing’s completion, I just want to clarify are those strap shingles. Just want to make sure I am correct because if I want to make sure I have the correct terminology if one of the lenders inquires about the roofing’s completion.

Hi Katrina,

Thank you for providing the photos and video showcasing the progress on draw 3's items. I appreciate your efforts in keeping us updated.

I have a couple of questions regarding the latest updates:

1. Do you happen to have a video or additional photos that display the drywall fully completed inside the property? This would give us a clearer idea of the finished look and progress made.

2. Regarding the completion of the roofing, I would like to confirm if those are strap shingles. It is important for me to ensure I have the correct terminology in case any lenders inquire about the roofing's completion.

Thank you for your assistance. Your prompt response will be greatly appreciated.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Write a response

Hi Myles,

Attached is the video for drywall.

Per the roof, because of the pitch it’s arch80 bri-roll, not shingles. On certain roof pitch you cannot use shingle but the material is the same is not how it comes. Subroof repair is done and new roofing material laid. The roof is 100% done.

Hi Myles,

Thank you for providing the video for the drywall. I appreciate it.

Regarding the roof, I wanted to clarify that due to the pitch of the roof, we used arch80 bri-roll instead of shingles. Although the material is different, it serves the same purpose and provides the necessary functionality. The subroof repair has been completed, and the new roofing material has been properly installed. Therefore, I can confirm that the roof is 100% done.

Thank you for your understanding. If you have any further questions or concerns, please feel free to reach out.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Thanks providing an explanation to help convey to the lenders in case any questions arise regarding that line -item. 

As for the video of the drywall, I think there may have been an error because there is no video attached. 

Thank you,

Hi Myles,

Thank you for your response. I apologize for the oversight. It seems there was an error in attaching the video of the drywall. I regret any inconvenience caused.

Please allow me some time to rectify the situation and resend the video promptly. I will ensure that you receive the correct video of the completed drywall.

Thank you for bringing this to my attention, and I appreciate your understanding. If you have any further questions or concerns, please don't hesitate to let me know.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Hi Monica,

Thanks for providing the video showing the work that’s been completed.  I can tell there has been a ton of work completed. 

I just had a few questions:
1.	In reviewing the line -item “Replace the existing roof to asphalt shingles” I noticed in the front of the house the shingles don’t seem to be installed. Do you have photos and/or video of the front roof shingles installed?

2.	In reviewing the line-item “New Front Fence” is the only fence you are planning to install in the front the white fence on the side of the property, or were you planning to install more fence directly on the front end of the property?

3.	In reviewing the “ceiling tape and paint entire interior of home” I noticed in the video that it looked like all the ceiling wasn’t fully installed. Also noticed some areas in the house were missing drywall and which I assume paint may be needed. Can you confirm if all painting has been completed with the roofing.

Hi Monica,

Thank you for providing the video showcasing the extensive work that has been completed. I can see that a tremendous amount of progress has been made.

I do have a few questions and clarifications:

1. When reviewing the line-item "Replace the existing
